title: Level 2.1
wikitext: {{CSS|User:阁楼福克123/Darkness.css}} {{Blurbackground|file=Nov9.png}} {{DISPLAYTITLE: Level 2.1: "The Subterranean Complex "}} {{Class 4}} '''Level 2.1''' is the first sub-level of [[Level 2]], being in between [[Level 2]] and [[Level 3]] == Main Description == {{Blurimage|file=Nov9.png|caption=An image taken of Level 2.1.}} '''Level 2.1''', otherwise known as "The Subterranean Complex", is a sublevel of [[Level 2]]. '''Level 2.1''' takes the appearance of a maze-like system of underground tunnels and natural-looking caves, all constructed with concrete bricks and other materials and structures related to concrete. These bricks appeared to be held together by a substance that appears to be a mixture of concrete and glue, making it nearly impossible to take the bricks apart. The temperature on this level can be somewhere around 87.8 °F to 99.6 °F, making traversing around the level sometimes impossible, due to the amount of heat rising in certain parts of the level. It is not known what generates the heat, but some theories suggest that the level actually has some sort of core in the middle of the level, similar to how the Earth from [[The Frontrooms]] has a core. {{Blurimage|file=RiverConc.png|caption=An image of a small concrete river, likely leading to one of the bigger areas of the level.}} The main light source comes from low-wattage light bulbs, grungy industrial lamps, and occasional torches mounted on the walls. These sources provide only minimal illumination, casting a dim, yellowish glow that leaves many distant corners shrouded in deep shadow. The flooring is an uneven mixture of packed dirt, smooth gray concrete, and occasional patches of loose rebar. Slow-moving rivers of wet, unset concrete are sometimes present within the larger "cavern" areas, posing a significant traversal hazard for the unwary traveler. The soundscape is dominated by the faint dripping of water, the low hum of distant machinery (which never seems to get closer), and the shuffling sounds of unseen entities moving just out of sight. The architecture itself seems to defy traditional physics in places, adding to the psychological disorientation. Corridors that appear to be perfectly straight can loop back on themselves without a single turn, and dead ends can suddenly become open passages when one turns their back on them for a moment. This non-Euclidean nature prevents the creation of reliable maps and contributes heavily to the sense of claustrophobia and being permanently lost. Supplies are almost non-existent; aside from occasional drops of [[Object 1|Almond Water]] that condense on colder pipe sections, there is nothing here to sustain a traveler. == Safe Zone == {{Blurimage|file=Safe2.png|caption=An image taken of the Safe Zone on Level 2.1.}} When one goes far off into the level, they may find an area known as the '''Safe Zone'''. This area acts as a safe spot in the level where a wanderer can rest for a bit. This area is accessed rarely through a door that appears randomly on a concrete wall, marked only with a bright red and white striped warning sign. It is a pristine, brightly lit, massive underground highway or storage area. The walls and ceilings are coated in a clean, brilliant white finish, reflecting intense fluorescent light that runs in continuous strips along the ceiling. The floor is smooth, black asphalt with faint white and red painted hazard lines. The air here is cool, fresh, and slightly metallic-smelling—a massive relief from the heat and dust of the main level. Entities seem physically unable to enter this zone. The Hikari (which is an entity on the level) cannot function here due to the continuous, pervasive light source that they cannot individually possess. It is believed to be a temporary sanctuary or a temporal anomaly. Long exposure here does not result in the standard dehydration of Level 2.1, and basic supplies (Almond Water bottles, protein bars) sometimes materialize near the walls. The exit is as arbitrary as the entrance. The doorway used to enter will simply vanish after an unspecified and inconsistent amount of time, leaving wanderers to find the next random exit that reappears, dumping them back into the dark, hot, concrete maze of the main complex. This transition back to the main level is always jarring, ensuring the traveler never gets too comfortable in the light. It is recommended by [[M.E.G.]] to stay in these safe zones for 4 to 5 minutes, as to make sure you get enough supplies to make it out of the level safely. == Large Caverns == {{Blurimage|file=Large2.png|caption=An image of a large cavern in Level 2.1}} Accessible only by following the course of one of the flowing concrete rivers into a large, dark opening in the wall, "The Large Caverns" is a vast, echoing section of Level 2.1 that resembles a massive natural cave system, but again, entirely constructed of rough, uneven concrete and mortar. The ceiling is high and jagged, with heavy, blocky stalactites hanging precariously overhead. This area is far darker than the main corridors, relying almost solely on extremely dim, widely spaced torches for light, which often flicker out entirely. The floor here is a rough, uneven, damp mixture of dirt and loose, fist-sized chunks of aggregate concrete, making walking treacherous. The concrete river that led you there continues to flow through the center of this massive chamber, its slow, sloshing sound echoing loudly. The Large Caverns are particularly hazardous due to an increased concentration of hostile entities, primarily [[Entity 8|Hounds]] and [[Entity 10|Skin-Stealers]], who thrive in the low light conditions and expertly use the massive, bulky concrete formations for cover while hunting. The [[Entity 8|Hounds]] operate mainly by sound and scent in this dim environment; their guttural barks and quick, erratic movements echo off the coarse, uneven concrete walls, making it nearly impossible to pinpoint their exact location until they are right upon you. The [[Entity 10|Skin-Stealers]], conversely, use the deep shadows and confusing rock formations for camouflage, often remaining completely motionless, mimicking the texture of the rough concrete itself until a wanderer passes close enough to ambush. This deadly combination of fast, auditory threats and silent, visual traps means that survival in the Large Caverns requires absolute silence and intense scrutiny of every shadow and corner. == Entities == The primary threat in this level is an entity known as The Hikari. The Hikari are incorporeal beings that possess the light sources within the level. They become aggressively active when approached, intensifying the light source to painful, blinding levels and attempting to disorient wanderers before making physical contact, which is usually fatal. The safest path through the complex is often found in the unlit sections of the maze, where the Hikari cannot manifest. However, the darkness brings its own threats. Other common Backrooms entities like [[Entity 8|Hounds]], [[Entity 9|Facelings]], [[Entity 10|Skin-Stealers]], and [[Entity 3|Smilers]] have also been reported to wander the darker corridors, using the lack of illumination and convoluted map to ambush prey. The cramped conditions make confrontation extremely difficult and often a last resort. == Outposts == There are no outposts on '''Level 2.1'''. == Entrances and Exits == '''Entrances''' *The primary way to enter Level 2.1 is by [[Phenomenon 1-A|no-clipping]] through a wall on an extremely hot section in [[Level 2]] '''Exits''' *Climbing a rare metallic ladder found occasionally leads upward to [[Level 3]] *Falling into a fast-moving river of wet concrete in a large cavern area can transport one to [[Level 10]] *Rare instances of finding a normal-looking wooden door set into the concrete wall can lead back to [[Level 1]] {{Audio|file="Bugs" Sound Level 92.9.mp3|volume=0.25|=true|loop=true}}{{Redirect|Prev=2|Next=2.71}} [[Category:Sub-Levels]] [[Category:Dangerous]] [[Category:Levels]] [[Category:Cluster I]] [[zh:Level 2.1]]
